reviewer.evidencePolicy: Mark only the deterministic helper packet contracts covered by checked-in CLI/runtime tests and review-prepare flow audit evidence.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim is a deterministic status-packet freshness rule, exercised by the app-level claim show regression test.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: TestRunClaimShowTreatsNonSourceHeadDriftAsFresh verifies generated claim artifact head drift remains visible while claim-source freshness does not block planning.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep claim-source freshness separate from generated artifact drift when changing status packet gitState handling.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names the review-input packet contract and the absence of LLM or review-application side effects, both covered by runtime, CLI, and flow-audit tests.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The checked-in review-input tests verify bounded clusters, skipped clusters, skipped claims, action-bucket focus, stale-packet rejection, evidence carry-forward, and no overrun into review application or eval planning.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep review-input packet creation read-only and deterministic when adding reviewer budgeting or clustering fields.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim is an eval-plan helper packet boundary, covered by runtime selection tests and the app-level plan emission test.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The eval-plan tests verify cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1 emission from reviewed cautilus-eval claims, skip non-ready and satisfied claims, retain evidence context, and classify app claims without writing host-owned fixtures, prompts, runners, wrappers, or policy.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep evaluate-claims planning as an intermediate packet producer unless a separate host-owned fixture authoring contract is introduced.
